From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actor (born 1929)

For the Australian rules footballer, see [Allan Miller (footballer)](/wiki/Allan_Miller_(footballer) "Allan Miller (footballer)").

For other people with the same name, see [Alan Miller (disambiguation)](/wiki/Alan_Miller_(disambiguation) "Alan Miller (disambiguation)").

| Allan Miller | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1929-02-14) February 14, 1929 (age 97) [[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Occupations | Actor, director, acting teacher, author, playwright |
| Years active | 1956–present |
| Spouses | * Anita Cooper (divorced) * [Laura Zucker](/wiki/Laura_Zucker "Laura Zucker") (m. 1976) |
| Children | 2 |

**Allan Miller** (born February 14, 1929) is an American stage, film, and television actor.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Allan_Miller&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Miller served in the [U.S. Army](/wiki/U.S._Army "U.S. Army") after [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") during the [occupation of Japan](/wiki/Occupation_of_Japan "Occupation of Japan").[[2]](#cite_note-LATimesGanesh-2) Noticing an ad in *[Stars and Stripes](/wiki/Stars_and_Stripes_(newspaper) "Stars and Stripes (newspaper)")* that was looking for performers, he began performing in shows to entertain the troops.[[2]](#cite_note-LATimesGanesh-2)

In 1948, after Miller returned to the U.S., he attended [Erwin Piscator](/wiki/Erwin_Piscator "Erwin Piscator")'s [Dramatic Workshop](/wiki/Dramatic_Workshop "Dramatic Workshop") at [The New School for Social Research](/wiki/The_New_School "The New School") in New York.[[2]](#cite_note-LATimesGanesh-2) He then studied acting under [Uta Hagen](/wiki/Uta_Hagen "Uta Hagen") (his classmates included [Geraldine Page](/wiki/Geraldine_Page "Geraldine Page") and [Charles Nelson Reilly](/wiki/Charles_Nelson_Reilly "Charles Nelson Reilly")); and under [Lee Strasberg](/wiki/Lee_Strasberg "Lee Strasberg") at the [Actors Studio](/wiki/Actors_Studio "Actors Studio") (his classmates included [James Dean](/wiki/James_Dean "James Dean"), [Marilyn Monroe](/wiki/Marilyn_Monroe "Marilyn Monroe"), and [Paul Newman](/wiki/Paul_Newman "Paul Newman")).[[2]](#cite_note-LATimesGanesh-2) In 1958, under Strasberg's sponsorship, he began teaching at the Dramatic Workshop. In 1960, he started teaching privately; one of his students was a teenaged [Barbra Streisand](/wiki/Barbra_Streisand "Barbra Streisand").[[2]](#cite_note-LATimesGanesh-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) In the 1970s he taught at [Yale Drama School](/wiki/Yale_Drama_School "Yale Drama School").[[4]](#cite_note-Strauss-4)

He is best known for his appearances on [television](/wiki/Television "Television"), including *[Kojak](/wiki/Kojak "Kojak")*, *[The Rockford Files](/wiki/The_Rockford_Files "The Rockford Files")*, *[The Streets of San Francisco](/wiki/The_Streets_of_San_Francisco "The Streets of San Francisco")*, *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*, *[Wonder Woman](/wiki/Wonder_Woman_(TV_series) "Wonder Woman (TV series)")*, *[Dallas](/wiki/Dallas_(TV_series) "Dallas (TV series)")*, and *[The Paper Chase](/wiki/The_Paper_Chase_(TV_series) "The Paper Chase (TV series)")*.[[5]](#cite_note-5) His film career included roles in *[Baby Blue Marine](/wiki/Baby_Blue_Marine "Baby Blue Marine")* (1976), *[Two-Minute Warning](/wiki/Two-Minute_Warning "Two-Minute Warning")* (1976), *[Fun with Dick and Jane](/wiki/Fun_with_Dick_and_Jane_(1977_film) "Fun with Dick and Jane (1977 film)")* (1977), *[Star Trek III: The Search for Spock](/wiki/Star_Trek_III:_The_Search_for_Spock "Star Trek III: The Search for Spock")* (1984) and *[Brewster's Millions](/wiki/Brewster%27s_Millions_(1985_film) "Brewster's Millions (1985 film)")* (1985). He has performed on stages across the country and on Broadway, most notably in [Brooklyn Boy](/wiki/Brooklyn_Boy "Brooklyn Boy").[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) He was producing director of the Back Alley Theatre, which he created and ran with his wife, Laura Zucker, from 1979 to 1989.[[2]](#cite_note-LATimesGanesh-2)[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) He was also one of the primary plaintiffs in a landmark lawsuit between [Actors' Equity Association](/wiki/Actors%27_Equity_Association "Actors' Equity Association") and Los Angeles-based small theaters, focused on the [Equity Waiver Plan](/wiki/Equity_Waiver_Plan "Equity Waiver Plan").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

He is the author of the book, *A Passion for Acting*, and a DVD, *Auditioning*. He wrote the play, *The Fox*, based on the [D.H. Lawrence](/wiki/D.H._Lawrence "D.H. Lawrence") novella, which was produced in Los Angeles, Off-Broadway at the Roundabout Theatre in New York City, and continues to be produced in the United States and around the world.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Allan_Miller&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Miller has been married twice. His first wife was actress Anita Cooper, now deceased.[[2]](#cite_note-LATimesGanesh-2) In 1976 he remarried, to [Laura Zucker](/wiki/Laura_Zucker "Laura Zucker"),[[4]](#cite_note-Strauss-4) who for 25 years was executive director of the Los Angeles County Arts Commission.[[2]](#cite_note-LATimesGanesh-2)[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Allan_Miller&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Filmography")]

* *[One Life to Live](/wiki/One_Life_to_Live "One Life to Live")* (1968-72 ABC TV Serial) - Dave Siegel
* *[The Rockford Files](/wiki/The_Rockford_Files "The Rockford Files")* (1975) - Michael Cordeen
* *[Baby Blue Marine](/wiki/Baby_Blue_Marine "Baby Blue Marine")* (1976) - Capt. Bittman
* *[Two-Minute Warning](/wiki/Two-Minute_Warning "Two-Minute Warning")* (1976) - Mr. Green
* *[Victory at Entebbe](/wiki/Victory_at_Entebbe "Victory at Entebbe")* (1976) - Nathan Haroun
* *[Fun with Dick and Jane](/wiki/Fun_with_Dick_and_Jane_(1977_film) "Fun with Dick and Jane (1977 film)")* (1977) - Loan Company Manager
* *[MacArthur](/wiki/MacArthur_(1977_film) "MacArthur (1977 film)")* (1977) - Colonel Diller
* *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")* (1977) "Shake Hands with the Man on the Moon" - Frank Devlin
* *[The Champ](/wiki/The_Champ_(1979_film) "The Champ (1979 film)")* (1979) - Whitey
* *[Cruising](/wiki/Cruising_(film) "Cruising (film)")* (1980) - Chief of Detectives
* *[Star Trek III: The Search for Spock](/wiki/Star_Trek_III:_The_Search_for_Spock "Star Trek III: The Search for Spock")* (1984) - Alien
* *[Brewster's Millions](/wiki/Brewster%27s_Millions_(1985_film) "Brewster's Millions (1985 film)")* (1985) - Political Newscaster
* *[Blacke's Magic](/wiki/Blacke%27s_Magic "Blacke's Magic")* (1986) "Wax Poetic" - Donald Rush
* *[Warlock](/wiki/Warlock_(1989_film) "Warlock (1989 film)")* (1989) - Detective
* *[Second Chances](/wiki/Second_Chances_(film) "Second Chances (film)")* (1998) - Dr. Rasmussen
* *[Bad Words](/wiki/Bad_Words_(film) "Bad Words (film)")* (2013) - Bald Glasses Judge
